title: Supply Chain Security
description: Software supply chain security protects the pipeline from code commit to artifact deployment using cryptographic signing, attestations, and dependency auditing.

sections.title: SBOM Generation
sections.description:
GitHub Dependency Graph auto-generates SBOMs for supported ecosystems (npm, pip, Maven, Go, etc.). Export in SPDX or CycloneDX format via GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/dependency-graph/sbom. Dependency review in PRs flags new vulnerabilities before merge. Use actions/dependency-review-action to gate PRs on license and severity thresholds.

curl -H "Accept: application/spdx+json" \
  /repos/org/repo/dependency-graph/sbom | jq '.sbom.packages | length'

sections.title: Sigstore & Cosign
sections.description:
Sigstore provides keyless signing via ephemeral certificates. Cosign signs containers and blobs using an OIDC identity (e.g., GitHub Actions workflow). The signing flow: Cosign requests a certificate from Fulcio (OIDC-aware CA), Fulcio validates the OIDC token from Actions, issues an ephemeral cert, Cosign signs and uploads to Rekor (transparency log). Verification checks the Rekor entry and OIDC identity.

cosign sign --oidc-issuer https://token.actions.githubusercontent.com \
  $IMAGE
cosign verify --cert-identity "$EXPECTED_IDENTITY" $IMAGE

sections.title: SLSA Framework
sections.description:
Supply-chain Levels for Software Artifacts (SLSA) provides a security ladder from L1 (build documented) to L4 (hermetic + reproducible). GitHub Actions supports SLSA via the slsa-github-generator which produces signed provenance attestations. L3 requires hosted builder and prevented source/tampering; L4 adds hermetic builds with no network access.

jobs:
  provenance:
    permissions:
      id-token: write
      contents: read
    uses: slsa-framework/slsa-github-generator/.github/workflows/generator_generic_slsa3.yml@v2

sections.title: Dependency Management
sections.description:
Dependabot automates dependency updates with version bumps and security fixes. Configure .github/dependabot.yml with ecosystem, directory, schedule, and reviewers. Use allow/ignore filters for version strategy. Dependabot alerts integrate with the advisory database and can auto-open PRs for critical fixes.

version: 2
updates:
  - package-ecosystem: npm
    directory: "/"
    schedule: { interval: weekly }
    open-pull-requests-limit: 10

sections.title: Compliance
sections.description:
Supply chain security maps to compliance frameworks: SOC 2 (CC6.1 logical access), FedRAMP (SC-12 cryptographic key), and Executive Order 14028 (SBOM + self-attestation). Enable private vulnerability reporting, require signed commits with GPG or SSH keys, and enforce branch protection requiring signed commits for critical repos.

git config --global commit.gpgsign true
git config --global user.signingkey $KEY_ID

details: Supply chain security addresses threats at every stage: source integrity (prevent malicious commits), build integrity (provenance), dependency integrity (known vulnerabilities), and artifact integrity (signing). The Sigstore keyless signing flow replaces the old PGP key management problem: in GitHub Actions, the workflow run gets an OIDC token from the id-token: write permission, Cosign presents this to Fulcio, Fulcio issues a short-lived code signing certificate tied to the workflow identity, the signature is stored in Rekor (immutable transparency log), and verifiers query Rekor to check the signature against the expected identity. No long-lived keys to rotate or lose. SBOMs should be generated at build time (not just from Git) to capture runtime dependencies. For SLSA L3, use the slsa-github-generator which produces an attestation in DSSE format signed with the workflow's OIDC identity. The attestation includes the builder ID, source repo, and build instructions. Dependabot's auto-merge feature speeds up non-breaking updates but should be gated on CI passing. For compliance audits, export the dependency graph data via the GraphQL dependencyGraphManifests query and archive attestations in a separate storage bucket with access logs.
cheatSheet:
Core Commands

  • cosign sign --oidc-issuer https://token.actions.githubusercontent.com $IMAGE — keyless signing.
  • cosign verify --cert-identity "$EXPECTED_IDENTITY" $IMAGE — verify signed artifacts.
  • curl -H "Accept: application/spdx+json" /repos/org/repo/dependency-graph/sbom — export SBOM.

Staff+ Patterns

  • Sigstore flow: OIDC token → Fulcio (ephemeral cert) → Cosign (sign) → Rekor (transparency log). No long-lived keys.
  • SLSA L3 requires a hosted builder with prevented source/tampering. Use slsa-github-generator.
  • Dependabot auto-merge for non-breaking updates gated on CI passing.

Watch Outs

  • SBOM from Git alone misses runtime dependencies. Generate SBOM at build time, not from lockfiles.
  • GPG-signed commits are not the same as signed artifacts. Commits prove authorship; artifacts prove build provenance.
  • Without a Rekor entry, a Cosign signature cannot be verified by third parties.

Interview Angle

Google/Microsoft L7: "Design a software supply chain for a regulated financial product." Discuss: SLSA L4 (hermetic + reproducible builds), SBOM with VEX (Vulnerability Exploitability eXchange), signed attestations with in-toto, and automated compliance evidence generation for auditors.
